title: DSA Cheatsheet - Hinglish
description: Data Structures & Algorithms ko samjho ek engineer ki tarah — sirf pattern ratte mat lagao, sochna seekho

concepts.name: Time Complexity Basics
concepts.explanation: Big O tumhe batata hai 'jab input bada hoga toh code kitna slow hoga?' Socho aise: O(1) matlab instant (pehli kitaab uthao shelf se). O(n) matlab har kitaab padhni padegi. O(n²) matlab har kitaab ko har doosri kitaab se compare karo. Key insight: socho jab n BAHUT bada ho jaye tab kya hoga.
keyPoints: O(1) < O(log n) < O(n) < O(n log n) < O(n²) — ye order yaad rakho!
Constants hatao: O(5n) → O(n) kyunki 5x slow bhi linear hi hai
Chhote terms hatao: O(n² + n) → O(n²) — n² dominate karta hai
Nested loops dikhe? Probably O(n²). Aadha hona dikhe? Probably O(log n).
Interview tip: solution batane ke baad hamesha complexity batao

concepts.name: Space Complexity
concepts.explanation: Space complexity = tumhara algorithm kitni extra memory rent pe leta hai? Socho tumhara ghar (memory). O(1) matlab sirf chabi chahiye (constant). O(n) matlab har item ke liye ek dabba chahiye. Recursion chupke se memory khata hai — har call ek plate stack karne jaisi hai jo baad mein utarani padegi.
keyPoints: Space = extra memory (input count nahi hoti)
O(1) = in-place, bas kuch variables — memory ke liye best!
O(n) = arrays, HashMaps, ya deep recursion banane mein
Recursion depth = space used (har call stack pe rehti hai)
Trade-off: kabhi zyada space use karke faster time milta hai

concepts.name: Complexity Comparison Table
concepts.explanation: Common time complexities aur unke performance ka comparison.
keyPoints: O(1) - Hash table lookup, array index access
O(log n) - Binary search, balanced tree operations
O(n) - Linear search, ek baar mein traverse
O(n log n) - Efficient sorting (merge, heap)
O(n²) - Nested loops, bubble sort
O(2^n) - Recursive subsets, brute force

concepts.name: Array Basics & Operations
concepts.explanation: Array ek parking lot jaisa hai jismein numbered spots hain. Tum seedha spot #500 pe ja sakte ho (O(1) access!), lekin agar beech mein gaadi daalni ho toh uske baad waale sab ko shift karna padega (O(n) insert). Fixed size matlab nayi jagah nahi bana sakte.
keyPoints: Array = fixed-size, numbered slots (parking lot jaisa)
Index se access = O(1) — ye arrays ki superpower hai!
Beech mein insert/delete = O(n) — sab shift hota hai
Index 0 se start hota hai, last index = length - 1
Dynamic size chahiye? ArrayList use karo

concepts.name: Two Pointer Technique
concepts.explanation: Socho do log ek sorted bookshelf mein dhoondh rahe hain — ek starting se, ek end se. Agar dono books ka sum chhota hai, left wala aage badhe (bade numbers). Zyada hai? Right wala peeche aaye. Ye 'chimta movement' O(n²) problems ko O(n) mein badal deta hai. Magic!
keyPoints: Do pointers: ulte ends se andar aate hain, ya ek hi direction mein alag speed se
SORTED arrays pe sabse achha kaam karta hai — pehle sort karna bhi theek hai
O(n²) brute force ko O(n) bana deta hai — bahut bada improvement!
Key patterns: two sum, container water, palindrome check, remove duplicates
Apne aap se poocho: 'Kya dono ends compare karke options hata sakta hoon?'

concepts.name: Sliding Window
concepts.explanation: Socho tum train mein baithe ho aur khidki se ghar dekh rahe ho. Har baar sab ghar fir se count karne ki jagah, bas jo ghar khidki se bahar gaya usse hatao aur naya aaya usse jodo. Yahi sliding window hai — elements ka ek 'window' rakhna aur efficiently slide karna.
keyPoints: Fixed window: size same rehta hai, bas slide karo
Variable window: right expand karo, left shrink karo jab condition tootey
Trick: sab kuch dubara calculate mat karo — sirf edges update karo!
Common patterns: max sum subarray, longest substring, minimum window
Time complexity: O(n) kyunki har element ek baar add aur ek baar remove hota hai

concepts.name: Kadane's Algorithm
concepts.explanation: Maximum sum contiguous subarray O(n) mein dhundho. Har step pe socho: kya main pichle sum ke saath continue karun ya fresh start karun?
keyPoints: Classic DP problem jo O(n) mein solve hota hai
Har position pe: current ko include karo ya fresh start
currentSum running sum track karta hai
maxSum ab tak ka best sum track karta hai
Sab negative numbers bhi handle karta hai

concepts.name: Dutch National Flag (3-way Partition)
concepts.explanation: 3 alag values wale array ko ek hi pass mein sort karo. Jaise teen rang ki gotiyan alag alag rakh do — ek baar mein!
keyPoints: Teen pointers: low, mid, high
low 0s ki boundary track karta hai
high 2s ki boundary track karta hai
mid unknown region explore karta hai
Single pass O(n) solution

concepts.name: Common Array Problems
concepts.explanation: Interviews mein baar baar poochhe jaane wale array problems.
keyPoints: Rotate: poora ulta karo, fir parts ulte karo
Missing number: sum formula ya XOR use karo
Duplicate: Floyd's cycle detection
Merge: two-pointer technique
Practice: Stock buy/sell, majority element

concepts.name: String Basics & Manipulation
concepts.explanation: Java mein Strings immutable hain — ek baar bana di toh badal nahi sakte. Jaise patthar pe likhi cheez. Agar baar baar badalna hai toh StringBuilder use karo — wo mitti jaisa hai, aasan se shape badal sakte ho.
keyPoints: Strings immutable hain — concat nayi string banata hai
Baar baar modify karna ho toh StringBuilder use karo
equals() content check ke liye, == reference check — strings ke liye hamesha equals()!
charAt() O(1) hai, substring() O(n) hai
String pool literals ke liye memory bachata hai

concepts.name: Palindrome Check
concepts.explanation: Check karo ki string ulta padhne pe bhi same hai ya nahi. Jaise 'madam' — seedha padho ya ulta, same hai!
keyPoints: Dono ends se two-pointer chalao
Non-alphanumeric characters skip karo valid palindrome mein
Center se expand karke longest palindrome dhundho
Odd aur even dono length ke palindromes consider karo
Manacher's algorithm O(n) solution deta hai

concepts.name: Anagram Detection
concepts.explanation: Check karo ki do strings mein same characters same frequency mein hain ya nahi. Jaise 'listen' aur 'silent' — same letters, alag order!
keyPoints: Anagrams mein same character frequencies hoti hain
Sorting approach: O(n log n) time
Frequency array: O(n) time, O(1) space — sabse fast
HashMap use karo anagrams group karne ke liye
Sorted string ko key banao grouping ke liye

concepts.name: String Reversal aur Rotation
concepts.explanation: Strings reverse karna aur rotate karna common problems hain. Rotation check karo: s1 + s1 mein s2 dhundho! Anagram of rotation bhi easily check ho jaata hai.
keyPoints: StringBuilder.reverse() simplest tarika hai
Rotation check: (s1 + s1).contains(s2) — O(n) trick!
Rotation check ke liye lengths equal hone chahiye
Word reverse: split, reverse array, rejoin

concepts.name: Pattern Matching aur Substrings
concepts.explanation: String ke andar pattern dhundho. contains() simple case ke liye, KMP algorithm O(n) mein kaam karta hai. Longest Common Substring/Subsequence DP se hota hai.
keyPoints: indexOf() returns -1 agar pattern nahi mila
contains() case-sensitive hai
All occurrences ke liye loop mein indexOf use karo
Longest Common Substring — DP se O(m*n)
KMP algorithm O(n+m) — large text ke liye best

concepts.name: String Reversal aur Rotation
concepts.explanation: Strings reverse karna aur rotate karna common problems hain. Rotation check karo: s1 + s1 mein s2 dhundho! Anagram of rotation bhi easily check ho jaata hai.
keyPoints: StringBuilder.reverse() simplest tarika hai
Rotation check: (s1 + s1).contains(s2) — O(n) trick!
Rotation check ke liye lengths equal hone chahiye
Word reverse: split, reverse array, rejoin

concepts.name: Pattern Matching aur Substrings
concepts.explanation: String ke andar pattern dhundho. contains() simple case ke liye, KMP algorithm O(n) mein kaam karta hai. Longest Common Substring/Subsequence DP se hota hai.
keyPoints: indexOf() returns -1 agar pattern nahi mila
contains() case-sensitive hai
All occurrences ke liye loop mein indexOf use karo
Longest Common Substring — DP se O(m*n)
KMP algorithm O(n+m) — large text ke liye best

concepts.name: Singly Linked List
concepts.explanation: Linked list ek treasure hunt jaisi hai — har clue (node) tumhe next clue ki jagah batata hai. Arrays jaisa numbered spots nahi hai; chain follow karni padti hai. Insert/delete mein mast (bas pointers change karo!), lekin '50th element do' mein slow (50 nodes walk karna padega).
keyPoints: Linked List = nodes ki chain, har node next ko point karta hai
Head pe Insert/Delete = O(1) — sirf ek pointer change!
End pe Insert/Delete = O(n) — poori list walk karni padti hai
Arrays jaisa random access nahi — nth element dhundhna O(n) hai
Jab frequent insertions/deletions chahiye tab great hai

concepts.name: Reverse Linked List
concepts.explanation: Linked list ke saare pointers ki direction ulat do. Interview mein sabse zyada poochha jaane wala question!
keyPoints: Iterative 3 pointers use karta hai: prev, current, next
Recursive end se start ki taraf reverse karta hai
Iterative space-efficient O(1) hai
Recursive O(n) stack space use karta hai
Sabse common interview question

concepts.name: Detect Cycle (Floyd's Algorithm)
concepts.explanation: Kachhue aur Khargosh ki race! Do runners ko track pe bhejo — ek 1 step chale, doosra 2 step. Agar loop hai toh fast wala slow ko lap kar lega aur dono milenge. Loop nahi hai? Fast wala end pe pahunch jaayega. Bonus: loop KAHAN start hota hai ye dhundhne ke liye, ek ko head pe reset karo aur dono ko same speed se chalao jab tak na milein.
keyPoints: Floyd's Algorithm = Kachhua aur Khargosh (slow/fast pointers)
Agar milein → cycle hai. Fast null pe pahunche → nahi hai
Cycle start dhundhne ke liye: slow ko head pe reset, dono 1 step chalao
Kaam kyun karta hai: math! Distance traveled cycle entry point se relate hota hai
O(n) time, O(1) space — koi extra data structure nahi chahiye!

concepts.name: Middle Node aur Sorted Lists Merge Karna
concepts.explanation: Middle find karna ka trick: slow/fast pointers! Fast ek dum aage jaata hai, slow middle pe ruk jaata hai. Do sorted lists merge karna recursively ya iteratively dono easy hai.
keyPoints: Middle: slow 1 step, fast 2 steps — fast khatam hone pe slow middle pe
Even length mein first middle milta hai
Dummy node iterative merge mein code simple karta hai
Merge Sort mein merge sorted lists use hota hai

concepts.name: Doubly Linked List
concepts.explanation: Doubly Linked List mein har node ka prev aur next dono pointers hote hain. Dono direction mein traverse karo! Deletion O(1) ho jaati hai agar node ka reference ho.
keyPoints: Har node mein prev aur next dono pointers
Insertion aur deletion dono O(1) agar reference ho
Dono direction mein traverse kar sakte ho
Singly se zyada memory use karta hai (extra prev pointer)
LRU Cache implement karne mein use hota hai

concepts.name: Middle Node aur Sorted Lists Merge Karna
concepts.explanation: Middle find karna ka trick: slow/fast pointers! Fast ek dum aage jaata hai, slow middle pe ruk jaata hai. Do sorted lists merge karna recursively ya iteratively dono easy hai.
keyPoints: Middle: slow 1 step, fast 2 steps — fast khatam hone pe slow middle pe
Even length mein first middle milta hai
Dummy node iterative merge mein code simple karta hai
Merge Sort mein merge sorted lists use hota hai

concepts.name: Doubly Linked List
concepts.explanation: Doubly Linked List mein har node ka prev aur next dono pointers hote hain. Dono direction mein traverse karo! Deletion O(1) ho jaati hai agar node ka reference ho.
keyPoints: Har node mein prev aur next dono pointers
Insertion aur deletion dono O(1) agar reference ho
Dono direction mein traverse kar sakte ho
Singly se zyada memory use karta hai (extra prev pointer)
LRU Cache implement karne mein use hota hai

concepts.name: Stack Implementation
concepts.explanation: Stack plates ki pile jaisi hai — sirf upar se daal sakte ho ya nikal sakte ho. Last plate in, first plate out (LIFO). Ye simple rule stacks ko 'undo' operations, function calls track karne, aur brackets parse karne ke liye perfect banata hai.
keyPoints: LIFO = Last In, First Out (canteen mein plates jaisa)
Saari operations O(1) — push, pop, peek, isEmpty
Java mein Deque (ArrayDeque) use karo, purana Stack class nahi
Perfect for: undo/redo, bracket matching, DFS, expression parsing
Interview pattern: 'baad mein kuch chahiye' = stack use karo

concepts.name: Balanced Parentheses
concepts.explanation: Check karo ki brackets sahi se match ho rahe hain ya nahi. Opening bracket aaye toh stack mein daalo, closing aaye toh match karo.
keyPoints: Opening brackets stack mein push karo
Closing bracket aaye toh pop karke match karo
End mein stack khaali hona chahiye
HashMap use karo cleaner matching ke liye
Indices track karo removal problems mein

concepts.name: Next Greater Element
concepts.explanation: Har element ke liye array mein right side ka sabse pehla bada element dhundho. Stack use karo toh O(n) mein ho jaayega!
keyPoints: Right se left traverse karo
Chhote/equal elements pop karo
Stack potential candidates store karta hai
O(n) time - har element ek baar push/pop hota hai
Stock span bhi same concept use karta hai

concepts.name: Min Stack aur Expression Evaluation
concepts.explanation: Min Stack O(1) mein minimum element deta hai! Trick: extra stack rakho sirf minimums ke liye. Postfix/Infix expressions evaluate karna classic stack problem hai.
keyPoints: Min Stack: extra minStack mein sirf minimums track karo
Min Stack: getMin() O(1) — duplicate minima ka dhyan rakhna
Postfix evaluation: numbers push karo, operator pe pop kar ke calculate karo
Infix -> Postfix conversion mein shunting-yard algorithm use hota hai

concepts.name: Histogram aur Trapping Water
concepts.explanation: Largest Rectangle in Histogram aur Trapping Rainwater — dono classic stack problems! O(n) solution stack se. Interview mein bahut popular hain.
keyPoints: Trapping Water: Stack mein indices store karo, not values
Jab bada element milta hai, beech mein trapped water calculate karo
Histogram: Monotonic (increasing) stack use karo
Dono O(n) time aur O(n) space
Interview mein bahut common problems hain!

concepts.name: Min Stack aur Expression Evaluation
concepts.explanation: Min Stack O(1) mein minimum element deta hai! Trick: extra stack rakho sirf minimums ke liye. Postfix/Infix expressions evaluate karna classic stack problem hai.
keyPoints: Min Stack: extra minStack mein sirf minimums track karo
Min Stack: getMin() O(1) — duplicate minima ka dhyan rakhna
Postfix evaluation: numbers push karo, operator pe pop kar ke calculate karo
Infix -> Postfix conversion mein shunting-yard algorithm use hota hai

concepts.name: Histogram aur Trapping Water
concepts.explanation: Largest Rectangle in Histogram aur Trapping Rainwater — dono classic stack problems! O(n) solution stack se. Interview mein bahut popular hain.
keyPoints: Trapping Water: Stack mein indices store karo, not values
Jab bada element milta hai, beech mein trapped water calculate karo
Histogram: Monotonic (increasing) stack use karo
Dono O(n) time aur O(n) space
Interview mein bahut common problems hain!

concepts.name: Queue Implementation
concepts.explanation: Queue line lagane jaisa hai — pehle aaya, pehle gaya (FIFO). Jaise bank ki line mein jo pehle aaya wo pehle counter pe jaata hai.
keyPoints: FIFO: First In First Out (bank ki line)
Enqueue O(1), Dequeue O(1)
LinkedList ya ArrayDeque use karo
Common in: BFS, scheduling, buffering

concepts.name: Priority Queue (Heap)
concepts.explanation: Ye special queue hai jahan priority ke hisaab se serve hota hai, order ke hisaab se nahi. Jaise hospital emergency mein — jo zyada serious, wo pehle.
keyPoints: Heap se implement hota hai (min ya max)
offer() aur poll() O(log n) hain
Java mein default min-heap hai
Custom comparator se max-heap banao
Used in: Dijkstra, A*, top K problems

concepts.name: Deque aur Sliding Window Maximum
concepts.explanation: Deque dono ends se O(1) mein add/remove karta hai. Sliding window maximum problem mein deque kaam aata hai — window mein maximum element O(n) mein nikalo!
keyPoints: Deque O(1) dono ends pe add/remove
Sliding window max: deque mein sirf useful indices rakho
Window mein fit na hone wale indices front se hatao
Chote elements back se hatao jab bada element aaye
Result O(n) — har element ek baar add/remove hota hai

concepts.name: Deque aur Sliding Window Maximum
concepts.explanation: Deque dono ends se O(1) mein add/remove karta hai. Sliding window maximum problem mein deque kaam aata hai — window mein maximum element O(n) mein nikalo!
keyPoints: Deque O(1) dono ends pe add/remove
Sliding window max: deque mein sirf useful indices rakho
Window mein fit na hone wale indices front se hatao
Chote elements back se hatao jab bada element aaye
Result O(n) — har element ek baar add/remove hota hai

concepts.name: Binary Tree Basics
concepts.explanation: Tree ek ulta family tree hai — ek purkha (root) upar, har node ke zyada se zyada 2 bachche. Koi cycle nahi, koi shared parent nahi. Key insight: zyaadatar tree problems recursion se solve hote hain — 'is node ko process karo, fir left aur right ke liye same karo'. 3 traversal orders master karo aur 80% tree problems crack ho jayenge.
keyPoints: INORDER = Left, Root, Right (BST → sorted output aayega!)
PREORDER = Root, Left, Right (tree copy karne ke liye)
POSTORDER = Left, Right, Root (tree delete karne ke liye)
LEVEL ORDER = BFS queue ke saath (shortest path trees mein)
Recursion pattern: base case (null), node process, children recurse

concepts.name: Binary Search Tree (BST)
concepts.explanation: BST ek aisi tree hai jahan har node ke left mein chhote aur right mein bade values hote hain. Isse search bahut fast — O(log n)!
keyPoints: Left subtree < node < right subtree
Inorder traversal sorted sequence deta hai
Average O(log n), worst O(n) agar ek taraf jhuki ho
Delete: inorder successor/predecessor dhundho
Validate min-max range se karo

concepts.name: Tree Problems
concepts.explanation: Common tree problems aur unke solutions.
keyPoints: Depth/Height: recursively children check karo
Balanced: height difference ≤ 1 honi chahiye
Diameter: kisi bhi do nodes ke beech longest path
Path sum: DFS running sum ke saath
Serialize: preorder null markers ke saath

concepts.name: Trie (Prefix Tree)
concepts.explanation: Strings efficiently store aur search karo! Autocomplete, spell check ke liye use hota hai. Search O(m) hai jahan m word ki length hai — number of words se independent!
keyPoints: Search/Insert O(m) — m word ki length
isEndOfWord flag se actual word aur sirf prefix mein distinguish karo
Autocomplete, spell check, IP routing mein use
26 children (lowercase a-z) — space O(26 * n) nodes

concepts.name: Trie (Prefix Tree)
concepts.explanation: Strings efficiently store aur search karo! Autocomplete, spell check ke liye use hota hai. Search O(m) hai jahan m word ki length hai — number of words se independent!
keyPoints: Search/Insert O(m) — m word ki length
isEndOfWord flag se actual word aur sirf prefix mein distinguish karo
Autocomplete, spell check, IP routing mein use
26 children (lowercase a-z) — space O(26 * n) nodes

concepts.name: Basic Sorting (O(n²))
concepts.explanation: Simple sorting algorithms — Bubble, Selection, Insertion Sort. Samajhne mein aasaan, chhote data ke liye theek, bade data ke liye slow.
keyPoints: Bubble: adjacent ko swap karo agar galat order mein
Selection: minimum dhundho aur swap karo
Insertion: sorted portion mein sahi jagah daalo
Sab O(n²) lekin insertion chhote/almost sorted ke liye fastest
In-place, stable (bubble, insertion)

concepts.name: Merge Sort & Quick Sort
concepts.explanation: Efficient O(n log n) divide and conquer sorting. Bade data ke liye ye use karo!
keyPoints: Merge: Stable, O(n) space, predictable O(n log n)
Quick: In-place, O(log n) space, average O(n log n)
Quick sort worst case O(n²) bad pivot ke saath
Merge linked lists ke liye better hai
Practice mein Quick sort preferred

concepts.name: Heap Sort aur Counting Sort
concepts.explanation: Heap Sort O(n log n) — in-place sorting! Counting Sort O(n + k) — linear time lekin sirf integers ke liye. Comparison sort ki limits (O(n log n)) se neeche jaata hai.
keyPoints: Heap Sort: O(n log n) time, O(1) extra space — in-place!
Heap Sort: Max heap banao, phir extract karo
Counting Sort: O(n + k) — k max value hai
Counting Sort sirf non-negative integers ke liye
Range bahut bada ho to Counting Sort inefficient hota hai

concepts.name: Heap Sort aur Counting Sort
concepts.explanation: Heap Sort O(n log n) — in-place sorting! Counting Sort O(n + k) — linear time lekin sirf integers ke liye. Comparison sort ki limits (O(n log n)) se neeche jaata hai.
keyPoints: Heap Sort: O(n log n) time, O(1) extra space — in-place!
Heap Sort: Max heap banao, phir extract karo
Counting Sort: O(n + k) — k max value hai
Counting Sort sirf non-negative integers ke liye
Range bahut bada ho to Counting Sort inefficient hota hai

concepts.name: Linear & Binary Search
concepts.explanation: Linear search: ek ek karke dekho. Binary search: beech se dekhke aadha hata do. Binary search sorted array pe kaam karta hai aur bahut fast hai!
keyPoints: Linear: unsorted pe kaam karta hai, O(n)
Binary: sorted array chahiye, O(log n)
Binary: search space aadha aadha karo
left + (right - left) / 2 overflow se bachata hai
Binary search bahut algorithms ka base hai

concepts.name: Binary Search Variants
concepts.explanation: Advanced binary search — pehla occurrence, last occurrence, rotated array mein search.
keyPoints: Pehla occurrence: milne ke baad bhi left mein dhundho
Last occurrence: milne ke baad bhi right mein dhundho
Peak element: neighbor se compare karo
Rotated array: sorted half determine karo
Sab O(log n) complexity maintain karte hain

concepts.name: DP Fundamentals
concepts.explanation: DP bas 'smart recursion' hai — same subproblem 100 baar solve karne ki jagah, ek baar solve karo aur answer save karo. Socho GPS jaisa jo routes yaad rakhta hai. Do approaches: TOP-DOWN (memoization) goal se start karta hai aur results cache karta hai, BOTTOM-UP (tabulation) chhote subproblems se answer build karta hai.
keyPoints: DP = Recursion + Caching (answers save karo, dobara solve mat karo)
Memoization = top-down (recursive with cache)
Tabulation = bottom-up (iterative, table bharo)
Pattern: 1) State define karo 2) Recurrence dhundho 3) Base cases identify karo
Space optimization: aksar sirf last 1-2 values chahiye, poora array nahi

concepts.name: Coin Change & LCS
concepts.explanation: Classic DP problems — interviews mein baar baar aate hain.
keyPoints: Coin change: dp[i] = amount i ke liye min coins
LCS: match ho toh diagonal se +1, nahi toh max of left/top
Edit distance: insert, delete, replace — 3 options
Base case: empty string ke liye i/j operations chahiye
Match ho toh koi operation nahi chahiye

concepts.name: 0/1 Knapsack aur Subset Sum
concepts.explanation: Classic DP problem — N items mein se select karo taaki weight <= capacity aur value maximum ho. 2D DP table banao. Interview mein bahut common!
keyPoints: dp[i][w] = i items leke capacity w mein max value
Har item ke liye: lena ya na lena — do choices
Subset Sum: Knapsack ka special case jahan values = weights
Space optimize kar sako 1D array se (reverse loop)
Interview mein: "Can you achieve target sum?" = Subset Sum

concepts.name: Longest Common Subsequence (LCS)
concepts.explanation: Do strings ke beech sabse lamba common subsequence nikalo. Subsequence consecutive nahi hona chahiye. Classic DP — backtracking se actual LCS bhi nikalo!
keyPoints: Characters match hone pe diagonal + 1
Match nahi hone pe max(upar, left)
LCS O(m*n) time aur space
Actual string nikalne ke liye backtrack karo dp table se
LIS O(n^2) — ya binary search se O(n log n) bhi ho sakta hai

concepts.name: 0/1 Knapsack aur Subset Sum
concepts.explanation: Classic DP problem — N items mein se select karo taaki weight <= capacity aur value maximum ho. 2D DP table banao. Interview mein bahut common!
keyPoints: dp[i][w] = i items leke capacity w mein max value
Har item ke liye: lena ya na lena — do choices
Subset Sum: Knapsack ka special case jahan values = weights
Space optimize kar sako 1D array se (reverse loop)
Interview mein: "Can you achieve target sum?" = Subset Sum

concepts.name: Longest Common Subsequence (LCS)
concepts.explanation: Do strings ke beech sabse lamba common subsequence nikalo. Subsequence consecutive nahi hona chahiye. Classic DP — backtracking se actual LCS bhi nikalo!
keyPoints: Characters match hone pe diagonal + 1
Match nahi hone pe max(upar, left)
LCS O(m*n) time aur space
Actual string nikalne ke liye backtrack karo dp table se
LIS O(n^2) — ya binary search se O(n log n) bhi ho sakta hai

concepts.name: Graph Representation
concepts.explanation: Graph nodes (vertices) aur unke connections (edges) ka collection hai. Do tarike se represent karte hain.
keyPoints: Adjacency List: Sparse graphs ke liye best
Adjacency Matrix: Dense graphs ke liye best
List: O(V+E) space, O(V) edge lookup
Matrix: O(V²) space, O(1) edge lookup
Weighted graphs weight bhi edge ke saath store karte hain

concepts.name: BFS & DFS Traversal
concepts.explanation: BFS (Breadth-First Search) paani ki lehron jaisa hai — pehle distance 1 ke saare neighbors, fir distance 2, etc. QUEUE use karta hai. DFS (Depth-First Search) maze explore karne jaisa — ek raaste pe jitna jayein utna jao, fir wapas aao. STACK ya recursion use karta hai.
keyPoints: BFS = Queue + level by level (paani ki lehron jaisa)
DFS = Stack/Recursion + gehraai mein pehle (maze explore karna)
BFS unweighted graphs mein SHORTEST PATH dhundhta hai
DFS kam memory use karta hai (sirf current path)
Dono O(V + E) time — har vertex aur edge ek baar visit

concepts.name: Dijkstra's Shortest Path
concepts.explanation: Source se saare vertices tak shortest path dhundho. Greedy algorithm hai — hamesha sabse chhoti distance wale node ko pehle process karo.
keyPoints: Greedy algorithm shortest path ke liye
Sirf non-negative weights ke saath kaam karta hai
Priority queue (min-heap) use karo
O((V+E) log V) binary heap ke saath
Negative weights handle NAHI kar sakta

concepts.name: Cycle Detection
concepts.explanation: Directed graph mein cycle hai ya nahi — DFS se check karo visiting/visited sets se. Undirected graph mein Union-Find ya DFS se. Deadlock detection mein use hota hai!
keyPoints: Directed: inStack[] track karo current DFS path mein kya hai
Back edge milna = cycle detected!
inStack node ko backtrack karte waqt false karo
Undirected: Union-Find simple aur efficient hai
O(V + E) time complexity

concepts.name: Topological Sort
concepts.explanation: Directed Acyclic Graph (DAG) mein tasks ka order nikalo — dependencies pehle aani chahiye. Course prerequisites, build systems, task scheduling — sab mein use hota hai!
keyPoints: Sirf DAG (Directed Acyclic Graph) pe kaam karta hai
DFS: Node finish hone pe stack mein daalo, phir reverse karo
BFS (Kahn's): In-degree 0 wale nodes se shuru karo
Kahn's: Agar sab nodes process na hoon toh cycle hai
Course schedule, build order — classic topological sort problems

concepts.name: Cycle Detection
concepts.explanation: Directed graph mein cycle hai ya nahi — DFS se check karo visiting/visited sets se. Undirected graph mein Union-Find ya DFS se. Deadlock detection mein use hota hai!
keyPoints: Directed: inStack[] track karo current DFS path mein kya hai
Back edge milna = cycle detected!
inStack node ko backtrack karte waqt false karo
Undirected: Union-Find simple aur efficient hai
O(V + E) time complexity

concepts.name: Topological Sort
concepts.explanation: Directed Acyclic Graph (DAG) mein tasks ka order nikalo — dependencies pehle aani chahiye. Course prerequisites, build systems, task scheduling — sab mein use hota hai!
keyPoints: Sirf DAG (Directed Acyclic Graph) pe kaam karta hai
DFS: Node finish hone pe stack mein daalo, phir reverse karo
BFS (Kahn's): In-degree 0 wale nodes se shuru karo
Kahn's: Agar sab nodes process na hoon toh cycle hai
Course schedule, build order — classic topological sort problems

concepts.name: HashMap & HashSet
concepts.explanation: HashMap ek jaadui library hai jahan tum kitaab ka naam bolo aur wo turant mil jaaye — O(1) lookup! Ye keys ko numbers mein convert karke (hashing) storage locations pe le jaata hai. HashSet same concept hai lekin sirf unique values store karta hai.
keyPoints: HashMap = key-value pairs, O(1) get/put/remove ke liye
HashSet = sirf unique values, O(1) add/contains/remove ke liye
Hamesha getOrDefault() use karo null check se bachne ke liye
TreeMap = sorted keys (O(log n)), LinkedHashMap = insertion order
Interview hack: 'fast lookup chahiye?' → HashMap/HashSet

concepts.name: Hashing Problems
concepts.explanation: Hashing se solve hone wale common problems.
keyPoints: Hash map O(1) lookups ke liye
Two sum mein complement use karo
Sorted string ko key banao anagrams ke liye
HashSet sequence checking ke liye
Prefix sum + HashMap se subarray problems

concepts.name: Permutations & Combinations
concepts.explanation: Backtracking matlab try karo, agar kaam nahi aaya toh undo karo aur doosra try karo. Jaise Sudoku solve karte waqt — ek number daalo, agar galat ho toh hatao aur doosra try karo.
keyPoints: Backtrack: explore karo, fir undo karo
Permutations: saare elements use karo
Combinations: k elements select karo
Subsets: saari possible combinations
Result mein add karte waqt hamesha list copy karo!

concepts.name: N-Queens aur Sudoku Solver
concepts.explanation: N-Queens: NxN board pe N queens rakho taaki koi ek dusre ko attack na kare. Sudoku: 9x9 grid valid numbers se fill karo. Dono pure backtracking!
keyPoints: N-Queens: Har row mein ek queen rakho — column aur diagonals check karo
Backtrack: Kaam na aaye toh queen hata do
Sudoku: Har empty cell pe 1-9 try karo, valid hone pe aage badho
Row, column, 3x3 box — teeno mein same number nahi hona chahiye
SAT solver bhi aise hi kaam karta hai!

concepts.name: N-Queens aur Sudoku Solver
concepts.explanation: N-Queens: NxN board pe N queens rakho taaki koi ek dusre ko attack na kare. Sudoku: 9x9 grid valid numbers se fill karo. Dono pure backtracking!
keyPoints: N-Queens: Har row mein ek queen rakho — column aur diagonals check karo
Backtrack: Kaam na aaye toh queen hata do
Sudoku: Har empty cell pe 1-9 try karo, valid hone pe aage badho
Row, column, 3x3 box — teeno mein same number nahi hona chahiye
SAT solver bhi aise hi kaam karta hai!

concepts.name: Activity Selection aur Intervals
concepts.explanation: Har step pe locally best choice lo — globally best result milta hai (always nahi, but greedy problems mein haan!). Activity selection, interval scheduling — classic greedy problems.
keyPoints: Greedy: End time ke hisaab se sort karo — jo pehle khatam ho usse pehle karo
Merge Intervals: Start time sort karo, phir overlap extend karo
Greedy kaam karta hai jab local optimal = global optimal
Activity Selection: O(n log n) sorting + O(n) scan

concepts.name: Jump Game aur Humare Greedy Problems
concepts.explanation: Greedy problems mein baar baar same pattern aata hai: maximum reach track karo, minimum steps count karo. Jump Game I aur II interview favorites hain!
keyPoints: Jump Game I: maxReach track karo — O(n)
Jump Game II: Level-by-level BFS ki tarah socho
Gas Station: Negative tank hone pe start reset karo
Agar total gas >= total cost toh solution zaroor exist karta hai

concepts.name: Bitwise Operations Basics
concepts.explanation: Bit level pe operations — bahut fast hote hain CPU mein. AND, OR, XOR, NOT, shifts — sab kuch. XOR ka magic: XOR karo khud se = 0, XOR karo 0 se = same number!
keyPoints: & : Dono 1 toh 1  (AND)
| : Koi ek 1 toh 1 (OR)
^ : Ek hi 1 toh 1 (XOR)
<< n: 2^n se multiply, >> n: 2^n se divide
XOR magic: a ^ a = 0, a ^ 0 = a
Bit check: (n & (1 << k)) != 0

concepts.name: Bit Manipulation Problems
concepts.explanation: Common interview bit problems — power of 2 check, set bits count, unique element. XOR se duplicate dhundho! Sets ke subset enumeration bhi bits se hota hai.
keyPoints: Power of 2: n & (n-1) == 0 aur n > 0
Brian Kernighan: n & (n-1) rightmost set bit clear karta hai
Single Number: XOR sab numbers ko — duplicates cancel ho jaate hain
All Subsets: 2^n masks — i-th bit set = i-th element included
Bit tricks O(1) space mein kaam karte hain!

concepts.name: GCD, LCM aur Prime Numbers
concepts.explanation: Euclidean algorithm se GCD O(log n) mein nikalo. LCM = (a * b) / GCD. Sieve of Eratosthenes se N tak ke sab primes ek saath nikalo!
keyPoints: GCD: Euclidean algorithm O(log min(a,b))
LCM = (a/gcd) * b — pehle divide karo overflow rokne ke liye
Prime check: sqrt(n) tak hi check karo — O(sqrt n)
Sieve O(n log log n) — N tak ke sab primes efficiently
Sieve mein p*p se start karo (p se ki sabse small multiple)

concepts.name: Fast Power aur Modular Arithmetic
concepts.explanation: a^b mod n nikalna competitive programming mein bahut use hota hai. Brute force O(b) — Fast Power binary exponentiation se O(log b)! Modular inverse bhi aata hai.
keyPoints: Fast Power O(log b) — binary exponentiation se
Odd exponent hone pe result mein multiply karo
Even: sirf base square karo, exp halve karo
10^9 + 7 standard modulus hai — prime hai toh modular inverse bhi kaam karta hai
Multiplication se pehle long cast karo: (long)a * b % MOD

concepts.name: Pattern Recognition Guide
concepts.explanation: Coding interviews ka secret: PROBLEM nahi, PATTERN pehchano. Zyaadatar problems kuch hi patterns mein fit hote hain. In clues ko pehchanna seekho — jaise ingredients se recipe pehchanna.
keyPoints: Problem padho → Keywords pehchano → Pattern se match karo
'Optimal' ka matlab usually better approach hai brute force se
Zyaadatar array problems: Two Pointers, Sliding Window, ya HashMap
Zyaadatar tree problems: Recursion (pre/in/postorder) ya BFS
Atka ho toh: 'Kaunsa data structure O(1) lookup dega jo mujhe chahiye?'

concepts.name: Data Structure Selection
concepts.explanation: Sahi data structure choose karna aadhi jung jeetna hai. Har structure ki superpower aur weakness hai. Problem ki zaroorat ko structure ki taakat se match karo.
keyPoints: Value se fast lookup → HashSet
Key se fast lookup → HashMap
Sorted order maintain → TreeMap/TreeSet
Min/max track → PriorityQueue (Heap)
Index se random access → ArrayList

concepts.name: Common Interview Gotchas
concepts.explanation: Interview mein common mistakes jo log karte hain — in pe dhyan do! Edge cases, overflow, null checks — ye sab interviewer dekh raha hai. Pehle loud think karo!
keyPoints: Long cast karo multiplication se PEHLE: (long)a * b
Array access se pehle bounds check karo
String comparison mein .equals() use karo, == nahi
Null check: node != null && node.val alag lagao
Modular arithmetic: cast to long pehle
Empty/null input edge cases hamesha pehle handle karo

concepts.name: Common Interview Gotchas
concepts.explanation: Interview mein common mistakes jo log karte hain — in pe dhyan do! Edge cases, overflow, null checks — ye sab interviewer dekh raha hai. Pehle loud think karo!
keyPoints: Long cast: (long)a * b — multiplication se PEHLE type cast karo
String comparison mein .equals() use karo, == nahi
Null check: node != null && ... — short circuit lagao
Empty/null input edge cases hamesha pehle handle karo
Modular arithmetic: cast to long pehle multiply karo
Off-by-one: < n use karo, <= n nahi (mostly)
